The Lawrence stock company will
give two performances on the even
ings of May 30 and 81 as benefit
for the Woman's club building fund.
Since the ladies Of the club have
planned to build home they have
been untiring in their efforts to
obtain funds, and the contribution
to be given by the local theatrical
company will mean a great deal TO
them.


The ladies' aid of the First United
Presbyterian church spent the after
noon yesterday at the Orphans
Home of Spokane sewing for the
children. Mrs. w. J. Mexican, presi-
dent of the ladies' aid, had charge
of the affair.


A special invitation is extended
to the Beta circle and Primrose
circle, camp 80, and Beta camp, w.
o. w.. to attend card party given
by the Spokane circle, No. III, Fri
day evening at s p. m. at 1913 First
avenue.


Prizes will be awarded and good
time is assured. Everybory is i'll
vited.


Mr.. and Mrs. F. F. Emery left the
early part of the week for Green
River Hot Springs, where they will
spend two weeks.


& & &


The Quilts, literary society of the
South Central high school, will give
the closing program of the season
Friday, May 20. at the school.


There is ripple of pleasant sur
prise concerning the engagement Of
Miss Doris N Damon and Gordon f.
Daggett, son of ex-Mayor Daggett.
The announcement has just been
made by Miss Damon's parents, DR..
and Mrs. w. II. Damon of 1920 Pa
cific avenue. The wedding will take
place in the autumn.


& &


Mrs. Susie Gallant and liver Mel
son were married Tuesday after
noon at 2:30 p. m. at the cathedral
of Our Lady of Lourdes. Father
Verhagen officiated. Mr.. and Mrs.
Nelson left Immediately for a we'd
ding trip to the coast.


4 4 4


The Spokane division. No. 222, Of
the auxiliary of railroad conductors
will give a card party Friday after
noon at o'clock in upper Pacific
hall. Refreshments will be served
and prizes will be awarded. The
committee on arrangements con-
sists of Mrs. A. ID. Mars, Mrs. J. II.
Reimers, Mrs. G.. F. Town, Mrs. G.
Menker. Mrs. o. Mcneil, Mrs. II.
Thompson and Mrs. F. M. Ross.

Mrs Nj. J. ROBBIE of 700 Spofford
avenue has as her guest her daugh-
ter, Mrs. b. l.. Daniels of Kellogg,
Idaho.

tury club held a meeting this morn
